sentiment.rationale: Warning of dangers and destructions decreed upon the waters, commanding travel by land; tone is protective but urgent.
Brief, reassuring revelation that the elders' testimonies are recorded in heaven and they may choose their own travel.
Strongly denounces sign-seekers and adulterers with fire imagery, warns of destruction, though ending with Zion's promise.
Commands forgiveness of all men with stern warnings about those who don't, addresses specific members' failings, promises Zion's establishment.
Grand declaration that the kingdom of God will roll forth as a stone cut without hands until it fills the whole earth.
Personal revelation answering William McLellin's secret prayers with missionary calling, though with a pointed warning about sin.
Challenges anyone to match the revelations, rebukes those who doubt, but promises the faithful will eventually see God's face.
Defines scripture as words spoken by the Holy Ghost, outlines bishop's calling, and commands parents to teach their children—instructive and encouraging.
Brief, calm assignment for John Whitmer to travel and keep the church history.
Appoints stewards over the revelations with accountability requirements—balanced between trust and warning.
Encouraging command to confound enemies through reasoning and persuasion, with reassurance that no weapon against them shall prosper.
Administrative revelation on bishop's duties—certificates, accounting, judgment—practical and organizational.
Brief, peaceful instruction to resume Bible translation while others continue preaching.
Gentle doctrinal explanation of Paul's teaching on children in mixed-faith households and the end of circumcision.
Missionary assignments with both encouragement and sharp accountability—elders who won't labor shall have it taken from them.
The grand Vision of three degrees of glory—celestial, terrestrial, telestial—the most sublime and transcendent section in the entire D&C.
Q&A format explaining Revelation's symbolism—the sea of glass, beasts, angels, sealing of 144,000—clarifying celestial mysteries.
Commands the united order for managing temporal affairs with promises of celestial inheritance and warnings about covenant-breaking.
Brief, encouraging call for Jared Carter to preach from east to west with the Comforter as teacher.
Brief missionary call to Stephen Burnett and Eden Smith with freedom to preach wherever they are received.

summaries.summary: The Lord warns of dangers and destructions decreed upon the waters, commanding some elders to travel by land while others proceed by water to their missions.
The Lord reassures the elders that their testimonies are recorded in heaven and gives them freedom to travel by water or land as they see fit.
The Lord denounces sign-seekers and adulterers, warns of fiery destruction, promises the redemption of Zion, and commands the saints to purchase land wisely.
The Lord commands the saints to forgive all men, warns that Zion will be established only through the obedient, and gives instructions on managing temporal affairs.
The Lord declares that the keys of the kingdom are committed to man and the gospel shall roll forth as Daniel's stone until it fills the whole earth.
The Lord answers William E. McLellin's unspoken prayers, calls him to preach, warns him to forsake his sins, and commands him to practice the laying on of hands.
The Lord challenges anyone to produce a revelation equal to the least of those He has given, and promises the faithful will eventually see His face and know He is.
The Lord declares that words spoken by the Holy Ghost are scripture, defines the bishop's calling through Aaron's lineage, and commands parents to teach children faith and repentance.
The Lord commands John Whitmer to travel with Oliver Cowdery carrying church funds, and to gather historical information for the church record.
The Lord appoints Joseph Smith, Martin Harris, Oliver Cowdery, John Whitmer, Sidney Rigdon, and W. W. Phelps as stewards over the published revelations.
The Lord commands Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon to preach and confound their enemies through reasoning, promising no weapon formed against them shall prosper.
The Lord outlines the bishop's administrative duties including judging worthiness, managing certificates for missionaries, and accounting for church property.
The Lord commands Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon to resume translating the Bible, while other elders continue preaching until the next conference.
An inspired explanation of Paul's teaching in 1 Corinthians 7:14, clarifying that children in believing households are holy and circumcision is fulfilled in Christ.
The Lord assigns missionary companionships at a conference, commanding them to preach repentance in all places and trust in the Lord for their families' support.
Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon receive the grand Vision of the three degrees of glory—celestial, terrestrial, and telestial—and the fate of the sons of perdition.
The Lord answers fourteen questions about the Book of Revelation, explaining the sea of glass, four beasts, sealing of 144,000, and the seven angels' trumpets.
The Lord commands the establishment of a united order for managing the church's temporal affairs, promising celestial inheritance to faithful stewards.
A brief call to Jared Carter to preach the gospel from east to west, promising the Comforter will teach him the truth of all things.
The Lord calls Stephen Burnett and Eden Smith to preach together wherever they can be received, or to send their testimony by letter.

crossReferences.note: Both teach that a wicked and adulterous generation seeks after signs; sign-seeking is condemned.
Both command unconditional forgiveness—'I, the Lord, will forgive whom I will forgive, but of you it is required to forgive all men.'
Directly references Daniel's prophecy of a stone cut without hands that fills the earth—the kingdom of God rolling forth.
Both teach that when men speak moved by the Holy Ghost, their words are scripture/prophecy.
Both command parents to teach their children to walk in truth, pray, and keep the commandments.
Both describe a vision of the Son of God on the right hand of the Father—Stephen in Acts, Joseph and Sidney here.
Both testify that through Christ the worlds were made and the inhabitants are begotten sons and daughters of God.
Paul's teaching on the glory of the sun, moon, and stars directly parallels the three degrees of glory described here.

verses.reason: 'Your testimonies are recorded in heaven for the angels to look upon; and they rejoice over you.'
'He that looketh on a woman to lust after her, or if any shall commit adultery in their hearts, they shall not have the Spirit.'
'I, the Lord, will forgive whom I will forgive, but of you it is required to forgive all men'—one of the most quoted D&C verses on forgiveness.
'Behold, it is said in my laws, or forbidden, to get in debt to thine enemies'—temporal wisdom on avoiding debt.
'The Lord requireth the heart and a willing mind; and the willing and obedient shall eat the good of the land of Zion in these last days.'
'The keys of the kingdom of God are committed unto man on the earth, and from thence shall the gospel roll forth unto the ends of the earth.'
'Verily, thus saith the Lord: It shall come to pass that every soul who forsaketh his sins and cometh unto me, and calleth on my name, and obeyeth my voice... shall see my face and know that I am.'
'When moved upon by the Holy Ghost shall be scripture, shall be the will of the Lord'—defines living scripture through the Spirit.
'Inasmuch as parents have children in Zion... they shall teach them to pray, and to walk uprightly before the Lord.'
'No weapon that is formed against you shall prosper'—echoing Isaiah 54:17 with Restoration application.
'Shake off the dust of your feet... as a testimony against them'—a solemn witness against those who reject missionaries.
'And now, after the many testimonies which have been given of him, this is the testimony, last of all, which we give of him: That he lives!'
'By him, and through him, and of him, the worlds are and were created, and the inhabitants thereof are begotten sons and daughters unto God.'
Paul's three glories interpreted: celestial = sun, terrestrial = moon, telestial = stars—the framework of LDS afterlife doctrine.
'He who is faithful and wise in time is accounted worthy to inherit the mansions prepared for him of my Father.'
